name: Grape & Kiwi Tart
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Almond extract OR 1/2 tsp Almond extract
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare and bake a 10-inch tart shell in a tart pan with a removable bottom. Cool on a wire rack.
2. While the tart shell is baking, prepare the filling:
a. In a heavy 2-quart saucepan, combine sugar, flour, gelatin, and salt.
b. In a small bowl, beat eggs and egg yolk with milk until well mixed; stir in gelatin mixture. Let stand 1 minute to soften gelatin slightly.
c. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and coats a spoon well (about 15 minutes). The mixture should be about 170°F to 175°F but do not boil or it will curdle. Remove from heat; stir in almond extract or almond liqueur. Cover and refrigerate until the mixture mounds slightly when dropped from a spoon, about 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
3. Meanwhile, cut each grape lengthwise into half. Peel and thinly slice kiwi. Set grapes and kiwi aside.
4. In a small bowl with a mixer on medium speed, beat whipping cream until stiff peaks form. With a rubber spatula or wire whisk, fold whipped cream into almond custard.
5. Evenly spoon the custard into the cooled tart shell. Arrange grapes, cut side down, and kiwifruit on the tart in concentric circles.
6. Carefully remove the side from the tart pan.
7. In a small saucepan over medium heat, melt apple jelly, stirring occasionally. With a pastry brush, carefully brush with melted jelly.
8. Refrigerate the tart until the filling is completely set, about 1 hour.
Variation: Dual Grape Tart - Substitute red grapes (seeded) instead of kiwi.
